**Abraham Lincoln High School** is a [public](/wiki/Public_school_(government_funded) "Public school (government funded)") high school at 2800 [Ocean Parkway](/wiki/Ocean_Parkway_(Brooklyn) "Ocean Parkway (Brooklyn)") in [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"), New York City. Operated by the [New York City Department of Education](/wiki/New_York_City_Department_of_Education "New York City Department of Education"), the school was built in 1929, and since graduated four Nobel Prize laureates.[[2]](#cite_note-Road-2) The current principal is Ari A. Hoogenboom.[*[as of?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*]

It was built during the [Great Depression](/wiki/Great_Depression "Great Depression"), and to save money, one set of blueprints was used for Lincoln and other high schools in New York City, including [Bayside High School](/wiki/Bayside_High_School_(New_York_City) "Bayside High School (New York City)"), [Samuel J. Tilden High School](/wiki/Samuel_J._Tilden_High_School "Samuel J. Tilden High School"), [John Adams High School](/wiki/John_Adams_High_School_(New_York_City) "John Adams High School (New York City)"), and [Grover Cleveland High School](/wiki/Grover_Cleveland_High_School_(New_York_City) "Grover Cleveland High School (New York City)").

The school features five gymnasiums, an outdoor [football](/wiki/American_football "American football") and track and field, a swimming pool, a photography studio, an [animal science](/wiki/Animal_science "Animal science") lab, an office classroom and an auditorium.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abraham_Lincoln_High_School_(Brooklyn)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The school was established in 1929 and named for former US president, [Abraham Lincoln](/wiki/Abraham_Lincoln "Abraham Lincoln"). From when the school opened its doors in September 1930 through the next 25 years, the school principal was Dr. Gabriel R. Mason.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In 1983, Dr. Jack Pollock, the principal, reported that 8 of 10 graduates attended college and/or university.[[4]](#cite_note-Dolan-4)

However, by 2010 C.J. Hughes of *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* reported that Lincoln High School had "struggled" with student academic achievement. In 2009, the school only had a 58% graduation rating. The [SAT](/wiki/SAT "SAT") averages for the school were 411 in reading, 432 in mathematics, and 401 in writing. The New York State averages during that year were 480 in reading, 500 in mathematics, and 470 in writing.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

In 2014 [Alan Bersin](/wiki/Alan_Bersin "Alan Bersin") announced the Mildred and Arthur Bersin Scholarship, to be awarded to students from [Gompers Preparatory Academy](/wiki/Gompers_Preparatory_Academy "Gompers Preparatory Academy"), Abraham Lincoln High School, and the [Preuss School](/wiki/Preuss_School "Preuss School") who are accepted into [Harvard University](/wiki/Harvard_University "Harvard University").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Admissions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abraham_Lincoln_High_School_(Brooklyn)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Admissions")]

As of the 2014–15 school year, the school had an enrollment of 2,325 students and 116.0 classroom teachers (on an [FTE](/wiki/Full-time_equivalent "Full-time equivalent") basis), for a [student–teacher ratio](/wiki/Student%E2%80%93teacher_ratio "Student–teacher ratio") of 20.0:1. There were 1,506 students (64.8% of enrollment) eligible for [free lunch](/wiki/National_School_Lunch_Act "National School Lunch Act") and 85 (3.7% of students) eligible for reduced-cost lunch.[[1]](#cite_note-NCES-1)

The school's racial composition is very diverse. African American students made up 38.3% of the school's student population, a plurality of the student body. [White](/wiki/White_people "White people") students made up over one-quarter (26.3%), [Hispanic and Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_American "Hispanic and Latino American") (of any race) students made up over one-fifth (21.1%), Asian American students made up 14.0%, and Native Americans made up the remaining 0.3%.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Extracurricular activities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abraham_Lincoln_High_School_(Brooklyn)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Extracurricular activities")]

The school offers many [extracurricular activities](/wiki/Extracurricular_activity "Extracurricular activity"), including Acting, [Animal Care Squad](/wiki/Animal_welfare "Animal welfare") [Anime](/wiki/Anime "Anime"), [Arista National Honor Society](/wiki/Arista_(honor_society) "Arista (honor society)"), [Cheerleading](/wiki/Cheerleading "Cheerleading"), [Chess](/wiki/Chess "Chess"), Chinese, Conflict Negotiation & Mediation, Debate Team, [Gay–Straight Alliance](/wiki/Gay%E2%80%93Straight_Alliance "Gay–Straight Alliance"), Guitar, Hiking, History, [Key Club](/wiki/Key_Club "Key Club"), [Yearbook](/wiki/Yearbook "Yearbook"), Library Squad, Lincoln Ambassadors, Lincoln Log (newspaper), Marine Lab Squad, [South Asian](/wiki/South_Asian "South Asian") club, [Weightlifting](/wiki/Weight_training "Weight training"), and Yearbook.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### Virtual Enterprise

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abraham_Lincoln_High_School_(Brooklyn)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Virtual Enterprise")]

The school has a virtual enterprise program where students create and manage their virtual businesses from product development, production, and distribution to marketing, sales, human resources, accounting, finance, and web design.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### Veterinary science

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abraham_Lincoln_High_School_(Brooklyn)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Veterinary science")]

The school has a veterinary science program in which students work with live animals.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### Internship program

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abraham_Lincoln_High_School_(Brooklyn)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Internship program")]

Students in the 11th grade can earn a credit by completing an internship with a private business, nonprofit organization or [government agency](/wiki/Government_agency "Government agency") that partners with the school. Students learn employment skills, develop professional relationships and receive professional advice.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### Athletics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Abraham_Lincoln_High_School_(Brooklyn)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Athletics")]

The school offers a variety of [varsity](/wiki/Varsity_team "Varsity team") and [junior varsity](/wiki/Junior_varsity_team "Junior varsity team") sports. These sports include basketball, baseball, [football](/wiki/High_school_football "High school football"), [bowling](/wiki/Bowling "Bowling"), [cross Country](/wiki/Cross_country_running "Cross country running"), [handball](/wiki/Handball "Handball"), track and field, [lacrosse](/wiki/Lacrosse "Lacrosse"), [soccer](/wiki/Soccer "Soccer"), softball, swimming, tennis and volleyball.[[12]](#cite_note-12) Lincoln varsity sports games were also [televised](/wiki/Television "Television") on City Gridiron.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In 2013, [borough president](/wiki/Borough_president "Borough president") [Marty Markowitz](/wiki/Marty_Markowitz "Marty Markowitz") and [councilman](/wiki/City_council "City council") [Domenic Recchia](/wiki/Domenic_Recchia "Domenic Recchia") funded a new $2 million fitness center at the school.[[15]](#cite_note-15) On November 27, 2018, the school along with alumnus [Isaiah Whitehead](/wiki/Isaiah_Whitehead "Isaiah Whitehead") commenced the opening of a new weight room.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Lincoln athletic director Renan Ebeid was recognized by All-Stars Teachers contest by [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball").[[17]](#cite_note-17)
